commit_velocity_per_day.description: Number of commits per calendar date, sorted by count descending
insights: Extreme burst pattern: top 3 days account for 43.9% of all commits (132/303)
Peak day (Apr 1) shows 53 commits — a marathon session
Zero commits on 29 of 33 calendar days — hyper-condensed activity windows
Average of 23.3 commits per active day indicates deep-focus binge development

commits_by_hour.description: Commit count by hour of day (24h format)
insights: Nocturnal pattern confirmed: 58 commits (19.1%) between midnight and 6 AM
Prime time is 9 PM: hour 21 alone has 43 commits (14.2% of total) — the single most productive hour
Late evening dominance: hours 20-23 account for 97 commits (32.0% of all commits)
Morning work pattern: hours 8-12 account for 93 commits (30.7%) — second productivity window
Afternoon slump: hours 13-17 only 37 commits (12.2%) — the dead zone
Bimodal distribution: peaks at 9PM and 11AM suggest two distinct work modes
The 21:00 spike (43 commits) is 2.3x the next highest hour — the witching hour

commits_by_day_of_week.description: Commit count by day of week
insights: Weekend warrior: 47.9% of commits happen on weekends — nearly equal to all weekdays combined
Sunday is the most productive day (85 commits, 28.1%) — the sacred day of code
Weekend-to-weekday ratio of 0.92 is extraordinarily high for any project
Tuesday is the least productive day (21 commits) — perhaps the week's lowest ebb
Saturday/Sunday combined outpace any 3 weekday combination

file_growth_at_era_boundaries.description: Repository file count at key commit era boundaries
insights: 208x growth from initial commit to first working state (2 → 416 files)
The 2180-file era represents a hoarding phase — importing archives into inbox
File count plateau at ~3414-3463: the codebase stabilized in this range
Net growth from era 2 to HEAD: only 1283 files added across 5 eras of intense development
The codebase reached its current density within the last 4 eras — a sign of maturation
Feature-heavy project: 49.2% of commits are 'feat' — building, not maintaining
Feature-to-fix ratio of 2.87:1 indicates rapid construction with moderate debt accumulation
Documentation investment: 10.2% docs commits — unusually high, suggests intentional knowledge capture
Refactoring is rare (2.3%) — the codebase was built fast and refactored in bulk
Near-zero perf commits (0.3%) — performance was not a primary concern during construction
The [I], [A], [F] prefixed commits from the Atelier era show pre-conventional-commit history
Essentially a solo project: Simon + Pastorsimon1798 = 99.3% of all commits
Pastorsimon1798 is likely Simon's GitHub username during a different auth context
Kai's single commit is the task-job series — likely an AI agent (Kai = AI assistant)
Kyanite's single commit suggests a brief collaboration or automated contribution
Solo developer density: the entire 303-commit history is effectively one person's vision

co_authored_by.description: AI co-authorship patterns in commit bodies
insights: Majority AI-assisted: 58.7% of commits are co-authored with Claude
Claude Opus 4.6 dominates the co-authorship — the primary AI collaborator
The 5 'Claude' (non-versioned) co-authorships are from earlier sessions before version tracking
True AI-native development: more than half the codebase was written in human-AI collaboration
This is a frontier metric — most projects have <5% AI co-authorship

data.message: feat(chat): add CreativeBrief builder from interview answers
Add workspace configuration and documentation updates
[I] Add all Atelier + Liminal creative outputs to inbox
chore: Feed inbox contents into compost heap, empty inbox
feat(guardrails): implement Deterministic Guardrails Framework Phase 1
feat(task-job): Rewrite src/index.js to use RalphLoop.run()
fix: GLSL validation too strict + ASCII prompt too complex
chore: Directory cleanup — feed junk to compost heap, digest, purge
Unify landing pages and add comprehensive dogfood QA report
feat: add comprehensive dogfood examples to landing page
fix: remove inverted quality gate that prevented Ralph Loop iteration
feat: Merge Hydra intelligence layer into Liminal (Phases 2-6)
security: comprehensive security hardening - red team audit remediation
chore: Complete rename from Atelier to Liminal
feat: Complete Meta-Harness integration with 18-type guardrail architecture
feat(task-job): Implement RalphLoop iteration engine
[A] Add generated gallery and output files
feat: enhance LLM generation with cache bypass option
Add P5GeneratorLLM with LLM integration
fix(ralph-loop): require code completeness before exiting iteration loop

insights: The 69,842-insertion commit is extraordinary — likely a lockfile/node_modules inclusion
Bulk commits dominate: top 5 commits account for ~147K insertions (30% of all insertions)
Inbox/heap commits (17K + 17K + 14K) suggest material hoarding before creative processing
Guardrails Phase 1 was a single 16K-insertion monster — built in one burst
The landing page iterations (13.5K + 12.8K) show obsessive polish cycles
Pattern of 'massive import → incremental refinement' is the dominant development mode

most_frequently_changed_files.description: Top 30 files by number of commits that touched them
insights: RalphLoop.ts (41 changes) is the beating heart — modified in 13.5% of all commits
The core triad: RalphLoop + index.ts + LLMClient.ts = 103 changes across 303 commits (34%)
soup-state.json (28 changes) reflects the evolutionary soup running frequently
landing.html (22 changes) reveals obsessive iteration on public-facing output
Test files appear prominently: ralph-loop.test.ts (12), cli.test.js (11) — testing was integrated
Binary artifacts in output/ and test-output/ being tracked is a repo hygiene issue
package.json (27 changes) = constant dependency churn throughout development
Net positive codebase: 293,834 more lines added than removed — pure construction
Insertion:deletion ratio of 2.54:1 — far more building than refactoring
Average 1,600 insertions per commit is exceptionally high — bulk operation pattern
484,812 total insertions across 303 commits suggests large automated generations
The deletion count (191K) is substantial, showing active code turnover — not just accumulation

longest_commit_streak.description: Longest consecutive days with at least one commit

insights: Longest streak of 5 days (Mar 19-23) coincides with the chat system + CreativeBrief build
Weekend-to-weekday ratio of 0.92 — this project doesn't respect weekends
After-midnight commits (19.1%) confirm a night-owl development pattern
The 5-day streak is the only sustained period — all other activity is in isolated bursts
13 active days out of 33 calendar days = 39.4% active rate — binge development rhythm
Prime time at 9-10 PM (80 commits combined) = 26.4% of all work happens in a 2-hour window

derived_narrative.developer_profile: Solo AI-augmented creator with nocturnal binge pattern
derived_narrative.work_style: Extreme burst development with multi-day sprints followed by dormancy
derived_narrative.ai_collaboration_intensity: 58.7% of commits co-authored with Claude Opus 4.6
derived_narrative.codebase_trajectory: Rapid expansion (2 → 3463 files) with late-stage stabilization
derived_narrative.development_philosophy: Build first, refine later — feat:fix ratio of 2.87:1
obsessive_patterns: RalphLoop.ts touched 41 times — the core loop is perpetually evolving
landing.html iterated 22 times — external presentation matters deeply
Sunday is the most productive day — weekends are sacred coding time
9 PM is the witching hour — 43 commits in that single hour alone
